sutta: 1 once the exalted 1 was dwelling near savarti ajita grove in anata pindika spa and there sumana the raja's daughter with a following of 500 royal maidens in 500 chariots came and visited him and after saluting him sat down at 1 side so seated she spoke thus to the exalted 1 lord suppose 2 of the blessed ones disciples were similar in faith similar in virtue and similar in wisdom but 1 was an arms giver and the other was not both on the breaking up of the body after death would be reborn in the happy heaven world but among the devas lord would there be a distinction a difference between them and the buddha said there would be sumana the arms giver when come to devastate surpasses the non-giver in 5 ways in divine lifespan beauty happiness honor and power in these 5 ways sumana the arms giver when reborn to a devastate surpasses the non-giver and sumana asked but if lord carrying on thence they should return to this state here that means to when become human lord would there be a distinction a difference between them there would be said the exalted 1 the arms giver as man surpasses the non-giver in 5 ways in human lifespan beauty happiness honor and power in these 5 ways the arms giver as man surpasses the non-giver but if both lord were to go forth from the home into the homeless life when gone forth lord would there be a distinction a difference between them there would be sumana and the exalted 1 said the arms giver when gone forth surpasses the non-giver in 5 ways he is often asked to accept a robe he is not rarely asked often to accept arms not rarely often to accept a lodging not rarely often to accept medicines for sickness not rarely and which whomsoever he dwells in leading the holy life such folk mostly act towards him with cordiality in their ways of living talking and thinking it is rare for them not to act cordially cordially to offer service seldom without cordially cordiality in these 5 ways an arms giver when gone forth surpasses the non-giver but lord if both win our hardship in that state would there be a distinction a difference between them in that case umana i verily declare that there is no difference whatsoever that is to say comparing liberation with liberation sumana said it is amazing lord it is wonderful lord how far reaching is the effect of giving arms and doing good deeds since there they are help and a boon to 1 as a deva a help and a boon to 1 as a man yeah a help and a boon when 1 has gone forth it is even so sumana it is even as you have just said that's the end of the suta
commentary: here in this utah you find the buddha is saying when we give arms when we do charity this merida follows us not only to the next lifetime it follows us for a few lifetimes and because of giving arms giving food to monks you attain 5 things you get a long life you get beauty you get happiness you get honored that means you are you are well known you are and the last 1 you have power these are the 5 things and so you find the buddha said now after you go to heaven you have these 5 things uh more than the person who is not an arms giver and after that you come back to the human realm as a human being you still have these 5 things surpassing a person who does not do so much charity and when you go off as a and you go forth as a monk later on in a future lifetime then you get a lot of offerings made to you whereas another person who seldom does charity when in the future lifetime he becomes a manga few people want to offer him things his blessings is not so much but however when he becomes an arahant the state of 1 other hand with another is the same the state of uh hanship so there's no difference that is why uh the state of narahan uh whether he's a pachirika buddha or a samas the liberation is the same uh that there is mention in 1 sutra where the buddha was asked what is the difference between a samasan buddha and his arahant disciple and the buddha said the is 1 who discovers again the aren't it full path and he walks the path he's very familiar with the path and he expounds the path he teaches the path to many and his disciples follow the same path after him if they follow the same path after him they will definitely reach the same place that's why basically there's no difference between an arahant and a samasan buddha
